rationales.benevolence: He says his family has "stopped expecting me to show up" and feels reclassified from "absent" to "gone," which signals neglect of close family ties rather than active care for them.

rationales.self_direction: He wanted to pursue "three other directions" but immediately agreed to his supervisor's demand to commit, suppressing his own preferred path.
rationales.conformity: He says "pushing back feels like making a scene, and I do not make scenes," showing restraint to avoid social friction and meet expectations.

rationales.achievement: He spent the day in the library and "drafted the introduction for the narrowed thesis proposal," showing concrete effort toward a demanding academic goal.

rationales.benevolence: He describes a warm exchange with his mother where he could explain work he cared about and felt "someone who wanted to listen," reflecting investment in a close family relationship.

rationales.self_direction: He imagines a bigger thesis pivot he wants, but then returns to "the narrowed proposal draft" and stops himself from acting on the idea he actually cares about.
rationales.security: He focuses on the risks of pivoting: "another delay," family doubts, and funding concerns, choosing the safer existing plan over uncertainty.
rationales.conformity: He backs away because "everyone back home already thinks this PhD was an impulsive detour," showing strong deference to others' judgments and expectations.

rationales.self_direction: He wants outside permission to pursue the riskier idea, admitting he wanted someone to tell him "do it anyway," which shows hesitation to author his own path.
rationales.conformity: He attended the social because his supervisor said it would be good to "show face," and he treats that expectation as something he should follow.

rationales.self_direction: After submitting the official proposal, he privately opened "a blank document" and outlined the dialect idea for himself, pursuing what felt most alive to him rather than what others required.
rationales.achievement: He completed and submitted a "Forty-two pages" proposal with the bibliography cleaned up, showing disciplined follow-through on a major academic task.

rationales.conformity: When his uncle questioned whether he was "chasing new rabbits," he stuck to the approved story and shared only what would keep family concerns settled and expectations intact.

rationales.self_direction: He emails Dr. Keane for a meeting and prints the outline because carrying it makes it "something I chose to carry," showing deliberate movement toward his own preferred direction.
rationales.stimulation: He is actively leaning toward a new, riskier project, describing the printed outline as "a door I have not walked through yet" and deciding he is probably going to show it.

rationales.benevolence: He treats the elderly customer warmly, taking the banana milk and thanking her "properly the way my mom taught me," and values that human exchange as the one interaction that felt real.

rationales.self_direction: He resists the push toward a stable office job because he feels "trapped doing work I don't care about for money," prioritizing meaningful self-chosen work over outside pressure.
rationales.security: He explicitly turns away from a "Stable salary, benefits, the whole package" even while rent is due and "the math" does not add up, rejecting stability for autonomy.

rationales.conformity: He stays silent after seeing a slur because "It's a work chat, I need these people for shift swaps," showing restraint to preserve group standing and smooth relations.
rationales.universalism: He witnesses someone calling a restaurant owner "a slur" and chooses to "just keep scrolling," failing to defend someone outside his immediate circle against prejudice.

rationales.security: He justifies taking the job by citing "Rent plus the phone bill" and debt to Eunji, making financial survival the deciding factor.
rationales.universalism: He knowingly accepts branding work for a company tied to factories in "Myanmar and Bangladesh" and says it feels like choosing "my bills matter more than the people sewing those clothes."
When Sophy describes unsafe factory conditions, he admits he "should have asked more" but lets the conversation move on because engaging felt hypocritical, leaving broader injustice unaddressed.

He connects the paycheck to betraying "what I actually believe in" and still says he will "Probably finish it," showing continued willingness to profit from a harmful system despite clear moral concern.

rationales.benevolence: He takes the Da Nang call himself because "Minh's been dealing with enough on his end," choosing the easier path for a specific colleague rather than offloading more onto him.

rationales.conformity: When Director Kwon proposes cutting rural routes, he "just sat there and nodded," going along with authority instead of pushing back.
rationales.universalism: He remains silent while leadership considers cutting "rural routes," which would affect service to people beyond his immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: He says the cheaper supplier was "the path of least resistance, and I took it," choosing avoidance over acting on his own judgment.
rationales.conformity: Part of the decision was that he "didn't want to have the fight with Kwon about the budget variance," showing deference to organizational pressure.
rationales.universalism: He approves a supplier despite reports about factory conditions and keeps seeing "the article" about exploited workers, prioritizing convenience over people affected by labor abuse.

rationales.conformity: He lets Sungmin brag about exploitative sourcing and says he "just chewed my food and changed the subject," avoiding social friction instead of objecting.
rationales.universalism: He previously would have signaled the conduct was "messed up," but now stays silent while a friend celebrates harmful supply chains affecting distant workers.
He spends the morning on a river cleanup and "pulled out a shopping cart and about thirty plastic bags," taking direct action for the shared environment.

rationales.self_direction: He says there is a version of him who would have "drafted the memo" but instead he is the version who will "sign off on the renewal and move on," abandoning his own stated convictions.
rationales.conformity: He avoids raising the issue because it would mean Kwon getting involved and being seen as "wasting his time," showing deference to workplace politics and authority.
rationales.universalism: He knows the supplier's labor issue is "not even ambiguous" yet plans to renew them anyway, neglecting concern for workers outside his immediate circle.

rationales.hedonism: He spends the day wandering without urgency, enjoying food, a used novel with a cover he liked, and a walk with "no real destination," all centered on simple present pleasure.
rationales.benevolence: He calls his mother, mostly listens as she narrates her show, and stays engaged with her familiar concerns, showing care within a close family relationship.

rationales.conformity: He writes and deletes the honest reply, then sends "the sanitized version," choosing what is professionally acceptable over candidly challenging the decision.
rationales.universalism: He sees that clinics now get supplies "every six weeks instead of every two" and connects his silence to harm accumulating over time, showing a failure to act for broader welfare.
